title: Voice House managed takeover and live-work beadification
description: Founder-authorized immediate custody activation. The custodian owns the local work ledger, inventories every live and dirty workstream, converts it into Beads, routes execution through explicit work items, and maintains the Building's Herdr surface. Preserve existing work; do not clean or reset it to simplify intake.

title: Bind all ongoing work to Building Beads and explicit Attempts
description: Every continuing worker must name this Building, an exact Bead ID, and an Attempt ID in operating context. Record legacy or unbound work as intake before delegating more work.

title: Operate and maintain the declared Building work surface
description: Keep the Building main Work tab live with local bv as the primary pane and the persistent custodian as the right-side management pane. Maintain Now, Attempts, and Evidence tabs without creating duplicate workspaces.
design:
## Ownership boundary

The authoritative Herdr Plus Building declaration and shared central generator remain Town/Infrastructure-owned. Voice House must not fork, replace, or independently mutate that generator. Voice House owns only local validation, acceptance evidence, and rollout after Town supplies an explicit routing Bead and Attempt.

## Declaration source

The Voice House surface must be derived from the canonical registry declaration rather than maintained as an ad hoc local layout. Building identity, exact workspace label, tab set, pane roles, split ratios, commands, and restart behavior must come from that declaration or its generated artifact.

## Workspace identity and focus behavior

Use the official exact-label focus-existing behavior. If exactly one workspace has the canonical Voice House label, focus/reuse it. Do not create a duplicate. Ambiguous duplicate exact labels must fail closed for operator resolution rather than selecting or creating another workspace.

## Default Work layout

The default `Work` tab is a 70:30 horizontal split:
- 70%: local work graph / `bv` primary pane
- 30%: persistent Voice House custodian pane

Additional declared tabs such as Now, Attempts, and Evidence follow the canonical registry declaration. Local rollout must not silently invent extra tabs or pane roles.

## Restart durability

After Herdr/server/client restart or loss of ephemeral terminal processes, the declared Voice House surface must be recreatable from canonical registry state, reusing the exact-label workspace and restoring missing declared tabs/panes without duplicating surviving resources.

## Preservation and rollout gate

Preserve the current `wC` workspace, its panes, dirty speech-core tree, and legacy `wC:p28` session until a generated candidate has passed local validation. No destructive migration, pane closure, rename, or replacement is authorized during planning. Rollout requires a fresh Town-routed Building + Bead + Attempt.
acceptance_criteria:
1. **Canonical source:** Evidence identifies the Town/Infrastructure-owned registry declaration and generated candidate used for Voice House; no locally forked generator or hand-maintained substitute is introduced.
2. **Exact-label reuse:** With one existing canonical Voice House workspace, applying/focusing the candidate reuses that exact workspace and creates no duplicate.
3. **Ambiguity fails closed:** With duplicate exact labels, validation/application stops with an actionable error and does not focus arbitrarily or create a third workspace.
4. **Default Work split:** A fresh recreation produces the declared Work tab with graph/`bv` as the 70% primary pane and the persistent custodian as the 30% secondary pane, within the generator's measurable tolerance.
5. **Declared tabs only:** Now, Attempts, Evidence, and any other tabs/panes match the canonical registry declaration; no undeclared local layout is silently added.
6. **Durable recreation:** A controlled restart/recreation test restores missing declared resources from registry state, reuses surviving exact-label resources, and produces no duplicate tabs, panes, or workspaces.
7. **Preservation:** The current `wC` workspace, dirty speech-core tree, running services, and legacy `wC:p28` remain intact until all candidate checks pass and rollout is explicitly authorized.
8. **Ownership boundary:** Shared generator changes are routed to Town/Infrastructure. Voice House evidence covers local validation and rollout only.
9. **Authorization:** No implementation or rollout occurs under the planning handoff alone; execution begins only with a fresh explicit Town-routed Building + Bead + Attempt.

comments.author: custodian.voice-house

title: Preserve and disposition dirty Talker MVP B edge
description:
Preserve and disposition the uncommitted Talker MVP B stream handed off by Voice House pane wC:p28. Scope includes `scripts/speech_talker_session.py`, `scripts/speech-talker-session.sh`, `scripts/test_speech_talker_session.py`, the Talker section of `README.md`, and the `.bv/` ignore addition. The edge implements mic/transcript → `pi --profile talker` → speech-out and barge interrupt triple: stop playout, cancel generation, truncate assistant history to the heard wall-clock prefix.

Evidence:
- `/tmp/organism-control-plane/voice-house-handoff-vh-xkl.1-from-wC-p28.md`
- `/tmp/organism-control-plane/talker-mvp-b-plan.md`
- `/tmp/speech-talker-20260717-031507/`
- `/tmp/speech-talker-20260717-031609/`
- preserved stopped session `wC:p28`

Known blockers/risks: live mic path and real barge triple have not been dogfooded; cut is approximate; durable pi session and real reasoner remain out of MVP B; direct commit is blocked until collision Bead vh-xkl.1.2 chooses a canonical file shape. Do not clean/reset/overwrite this dirty tree.

title: Resolve Talker versus Hearth voice-front-door collision
description:
Resolve the collision between the dirty Talker MVP B stream and the existing clean Hearth voice-front-door v0 candidate before either is integrated. Candidate branch `feat/hearth-voice-front-door-v0-20260717T020754Z-e186d147` at `d61667d` contains commits `c5941ee`, `fe81563`, and `d61667d`, all based on `6e88ef0`, and modifies the same four paths: `README.md`, `scripts/speech-talker-session.sh`, `scripts/speech_talker_session.py`, `scripts/test_speech_talker_session.py`.

The candidate adds Overseer target-pane dispatch, Herdr session JSONL extraction, remote SSH control, preflight/fail-closed handling, and 86 tests. The dirty stream is the smaller local Talker/profile edge. Decide merge, split into separate entrypoints, or park one stream; preserve both until accepted. No merge to main without Founder direction.

Acceptance: one documented canonical ownership/entrypoint decision; both sources preserved; no silent overwrite; applicable tests and deployment dependencies identified; integration route and branch policy recorded.

title: Custody and reproduce the shared Talker profile and stub tools
description:
Place the live shared Talker profile and reasoner-tool stubs under explicit custody and make their installation/reproduction truthful. Current artifacts are outside speech-core git:
- `/home/sf/workspace/.pi/profiles/talker/`
- `/home/sf/workspace/.pi/profiles/_shared/extensions/talker-reasoner-tools/`
- `talker:` entry in `/home/sf/workspace/.pi/profiles/profile-manifest.yaml`
- runtime stub evidence `~/.local/state/speech-core/talker/reasoner-stubs.jsonl`

The profile uses `cpa/deepseek-v4-flash`; `ask_reasoner` and `send_to_reasoner` are TypeScript stubs, not a live reasoner wire. Decide the authoritative repository/installer without mutating another Building's graph. Acceptance: ownership and source-of-truth are documented; a fresh install path is reproducible; stub status is explicit; runtime sessions/state are not accidentally versioned.

title: Live-dogfood the canonical Talker voice loop and barge triple
description:
After the Talker/front-door collision is resolved and a canonical edge is selected, verify the local Talker path end to end on the intended mic/client host: microphone → speech-core transcript commit → Talker generation → speech-out, including the interrupt triple (stop playout, cancel in-flight generation where safe, truncate history to the heard prefix).

Existing synthetic evidence proves only `--once-text` Talker → speech-out rc=0 and four helper tests. Mic adapter currently resolves from `target/release/speech-core-mic-adapter`; services at 8765 and 8788 are live. Acceptance: preserved run directory/event log, mic transcript, audible reply, and a barge event showing each applicable leg and the selected cut source/fallback. No service disruption during intake.

title: Wire precise CTC heard-prefix cuts into the canonical Talker edge
description:
Track follow-up to replace or refine Talker's wall-clock-only history cut with the existing warm CTC alignment path, with explicit fallback. Current Talker MVP B uses approximate words-per-second truncation. Existing branch work has `scripts/barge_in_align/align_worker.py`, a live TCP worker on port 8791, and CTC cut history; CUPE remains experimental/parked and must not silently become the default.

Acceptance: chosen protocol and ownership documented; CTC cut used when healthy; wall-clock fallback explicit; event evidence records cut source/confidence; tests cover unavailable/timeout behavior. Depends on canonical Talker/front-door disposition and successful live dogfood.

title: Replace Talker reasoner stubs with an authorized real wire
description:
Future MVP C work: replace `ask_reasoner` / `send_to_reasoner` logging stubs with a real, authorized delivery contract to the primary reasoner session. The Talker must remain a front layer, require clear natural-language approval before dispatch, never claim stub output as a reasoner result, and preserve cancellation/history semantics.

Acceptance: explicit target identity and authorization; delivery acknowledgement; no duplicate dispatch; fail-closed behavior on missing/changed target; observable provenance; tests. This is intentionally deferred until MVP B ownership, profile custody, and front-door collision are resolved.

title: Inventory and govern live Voice House speech services
description:
Own the operational inventory and health of the currently live Voice House speech stack without disrupting it. Observed services: speech-core A :8765, speech-core B :8766 (running deleted older executable), speech-out :8788, Supertonic :7788, warm CTC align worker :8791, and experimental CUPE worker :8792. Systemd units and environment files are under `~/.config/systemd/user/` and `~/.config/speech-core/`.

Acceptance: map each process to installed binary/build provenance and intended product path; record restart/upgrade policy; investigate repeated incomplete WebSocket handshakes without assuming outage; decide whether standalone/transient CTC/CUPE workers are intentional. Preserve all running services until a separately approved operational Attempt.

title: Set retention and git policy for Beads and terminal checkpoints
description:
Classify and protect non-product local custody artifacts currently untracked in speech-core. `.beads/` is the authoritative Voice House work ledger and has an active `bv` reader. `.checkpoints/` contains Pi terminal-settlement checkpoint triples from multiple sessions; it is not a Beads graph snapshot and must not be deleted or casually committed. Current `br doctor` reports healthy DB/JSONL sync but warns about incomplete internal ignore expectations and old empty lock mtimes while `bv` is live.

Acceptance: documented source/owner/retention and git policy for `.beads/` and `.checkpoints/`; no runtime DB/lock or session payload accidentally committed; authoritative ledger remains readable and synchronized; unknown artifacts preserved.

title: Verify and disposition committed assistant self-ASR legacy stt-pipe branch
description:
Preserve, verify, and disposition the committed `feature/assistant-self-asr` branch as the current successor to legacy `stt-pipe` voice work. The branch is at `6e88ef0`, matches `origin/feature/assistant-self-asr`, and is roughly 42 commits ahead of local `main`. It contains dual-Nemotron assistant self-ASR, live speech/TTS barge-in, CTC forced alignment and warm-worker paths, transcript/finalize fixes, and TUI cut rendering. Clean feature worktrees for ASR eval, dual live, and STS-peek are ancestors of this branch.

No live filesystem or process named `stt-pipe` was found; the only current direct reference labels it legacy beside Speech Core. Treat `speech-core` as the active Voice House implementation unless the Overseer supplies another owner/source. The working tree is dirty only because Talker/custody artifacts overlay this committed branch, so no checkout/rebase/merge is safe until those are preserved and dispositioned.

Acceptance: committed scope and test/evidence map reviewed; current installed daemons/binaries mapped to a build; branch-versus-main/origin policy recorded; dirty overlay preserved; explicit Founder/Town decision obtained before any merge to main. Intake-only: future execution needs a fresh Building+Bead+Attempt and vh-xkl.2 binding.
